**NEXUS**

6-Week Build Plan

*Detailed Task Breakdown · March to May 2026*

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  Rule: At the end of each week, there must be ONE concrete deliverable
  you can run and show someone. If a week ends with nothing working, stop
  and debug before moving forward.

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| **Week 1: Outlook Integration + Project Setup**                       |
|                                                                       |
| *Goal: Get real emails flowing into your pipeline from your college   |
| Outlook account.*                                                     |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

**Tasks**

-   Set up Node.js + Express backend project structure

-   Register Microsoft Azure app --- get Client ID and Secret

-   Implement OAuth 2.0 login flow for Outlook (MS Graph API)

-   Write outlook.js connector --- fetch last 50 unread messages

-   Set up Firebase Firestore --- create messages collection

-   Store raw fetched emails in Firestore with status: \'raw\'

-   Write basic deduplication using SHA-256 hash

-   Test: Fetch 10 real emails from your college Outlook

  --------------- ---------------------------------------------------------
  **✓             Real emails from your IIT Guwahati Outlook stored in
  Deliverable**   Firestore.

  **⚠ Risk**      Azure app registration requires a Microsoft account ---
                  do this on Day 1.
  --------------- ---------------------------------------------------------

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| **Week 2: AI Pipeline --- Extract, Score, Classify**                  |
|                                                                       |
| *Goal: Turn raw email text into structured, prioritised data using    |
| Groq.*                                                                |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

**Tasks**

-   Set up Groq API account --- get free API key

-   Write extraction prompt --- parse dates, tasks, amounts from email
    text

-   Write classification prompt --- assign category and confidence score

-   Write summarisation prompt --- 2-sentence plain-English summary

-   Build score.js --- implement the priority scoring formula from PRD

-   Build classify.js --- map score to Critical / Important / Normal /
    Low

-   Route low-confidence items (\< 70%) to \'review\' status

-   Test pipeline on 20 real emails --- check accuracy manually

  --------------- ---------------------------------------------------------
  **✓             Every stored email has priority, category, extractedTask,
  Deliverable**   extractedDate, and confidence populated.

  **⚠ Risk**      LLM outputs inconsistent JSON --- add a retry + fallback
                  parser.
  --------------- ---------------------------------------------------------

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| **Week 3: ERP Scraper + WhatsApp Prototype**                          |
|                                                                       |
| *Goal: Add the remaining two data sources --- ERP notices and         |
| selected WhatsApp chats.*                                             |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

**Tasks**

-   Build erp.js with Puppeteer --- scrape 3 pages: notices, fee status,
    exam schedule

-   Handle ERP login session --- persist cookies to avoid re-login every
    time

-   Run ERP scraper output through the same AI pipeline from Week 2

-   Set up whatsapp-web.js --- scan QR code to link your number

-   Implement selective chat monitoring --- store watched chat IDs in
    Firebase settings

-   Test WhatsApp message ingestion from 2--3 selected groups

-   Write whatsappAlert.js --- send self-message on Critical item
    detection

-   Set up cron jobs (node-cron) --- Outlook every 15 min, ERP every 30
    min

  --------------- ---------------------------------------------------------
  **✓             All three sources feeding into the pipeline. WhatsApp
  Deliverable**   alert demonstrable in a video.

  **⚠ Risk**      ERP login may fail behind VPN. Test on college WiFi.
                  WhatsApp may need re-auth after restarts.
  --------------- ---------------------------------------------------------

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| **Week 4: React Frontend --- Newspaper Dashboard**                    |
|                                                                       |
| *Goal: Build the full newspaper-style dashboard that displays all     |
| processed data.*                                                      |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

**Tasks**

-   Set up React + Vite frontend project

-   Build Masthead component --- date, sources, last updated time

-   Build TodaysBriefing component --- lead story + expandable story
    cards

-   Build TodaysSchedule component --- timeline view of day\'s events

-   Build DeadlineTracker component --- grouped by Today / Tomorrow / In
    X Days

-   Build NeedsReview component --- uncertain items with manual deadline
    input

-   Write useMessages hook --- polls /api/messages every 5 minutes

-   Make entire layout mobile-responsive with Tailwind CSS

  --------------- ---------------------------------------------------------
  **✓             Full dashboard rendering real data from your backend.
  Deliverable**   Works on mobile and desktop.

  **⚠ Risk**      Mobile layout requires careful testing --- use Chrome
                  DevTools device emulator throughout.
  --------------- ---------------------------------------------------------

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| **Week 5: Connect Everything + Deploy**                               |
|                                                                       |
| *Goal: Get the full system running end-to-end and deployed live.*     |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

**Tasks**

-   Connect frontend to backend API --- replace mock data with real
    /api/messages

-   Implement Firebase Auth --- Google login for the frontend

-   Wire user settings --- WhatsApp number, watched chats, digest time

-   Deploy backend to Railway --- set all environment variables in
    Railway dashboard

-   Deploy frontend to Vercel --- connect to Railway backend URL

-   Set up .env.example file with all required variables (no real
    values)

-   Test full flow: email arrives → processed → appears on dashboard →
    WhatsApp alert

-   Fix any CORS or authentication issues between frontend and backend

  --------------- ---------------------------------------------------------
  **✓             Live URL accessible from any device. Full pipeline
  Deliverable**   running in production.

  **⚠ Risk**      Railway free tier sleeps after inactivity --- upgrade to
                  paid (\$5/month) for always-on cron jobs.
  --------------- ---------------------------------------------------------

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| **Week 6: Polish, README, Demo Video**                                |
|                                                                       |
| *Goal: Make the project recruiter-ready. Every detail matters for CV  |
| and interviews.*                                                      |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

**Tasks**

-   Write a comprehensive README --- what it does, architecture diagram,
    setup instructions

-   Add screenshots of all 4 dashboard sections to README

-   Record a 3-minute demo video --- show full flow from email arrival
    to WhatsApp alert

-   Add architecture diagram (draw.io or Excalidraw) to README

-   Write KNOWN_LIMITATIONS.md --- honest list of what works and what
    doesn\'t

-   Clean up code --- remove console.logs, add comments on complex
    sections

-   Update GitHub repo description and add relevant topics/tags

-   Update portfolio website with Nexus --- headline project with live
    link

  --------------- ---------------------------------------------------------
  **✓             GitHub repo ready to share with any recruiter. Live demo
  Deliverable**   accessible.

  **⚠ Risk**      None --- this is polish work. Start early if Week 5 runs
                  over.
  --------------- ---------------------------------------------------------

**Final Checklist Before CV Submission**

-   Live URL works from any device without login (or with a test account
    provided)

-   GitHub README has architecture diagram, screenshots, and setup
    instructions

-   Demo video (3 min) shows full pipeline from email arrival to
    WhatsApp alert

-   KNOWN_LIMITATIONS.md is honest about what is prototype vs
    production-ready

-   You can explain every line of the classification algorithm from
    memory

-   You have answers prepared for: Why did you build this? How does it
    classify? What if it misses something? How is it different from just
    checking email?

**NEXUS**

Technical Architecture Document

*System Design, Stack Decisions & Data Flow*

**1. System Overview**

Nexus is a full-stack web application with three main layers: a data
ingestion backend, an AI processing pipeline, and a React frontend
dashboard. The backend runs continuously on Railway (always-on server),
while the frontend is deployed on Vercel.

**1.1 High-Level Architecture**

  --------------- --------------------------- ----------------------------
  **Layer**       **Technology**              **Responsibility**

  Frontend        React + Vite (Vercel)       Dashboard UI,
                                              mobile-responsive newspaper
                                              layout

  Backend         Node.js + Express (Railway) API server, cron jobs,
                                              pipeline orchestration

  Data Ingestion  MS Graph API,               Fetching raw messages from
                  whatsapp-web.js, Puppeteer  all sources

  AI Processing   Groq API (LLaMA 3)          Classification,
                                              summarisation, deadline
                                              extraction

  Database        Firebase Firestore          Storing processed messages,
                                              user settings, deadlines

  Auth            Firebase Auth + MS OAuth    User login, Outlook
                  2.0                         permission scopes

  Alerts          whatsapp-web.js             Sending critical alerts to
                  (self-message)              user\'s WhatsApp
  --------------- --------------------------- ----------------------------

**2. Backend Architecture**

**2.1 Directory Structure**

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  nexus-backend/ ├── connectors/ │ ├── outlook.js // MS Graph API
  integration │ ├── whatsapp.js // whatsapp-web.js session manager │ └──
  erp.js // Puppeteer scraper ├── pipeline/ │ ├── dedup.js // Hash-based
  deduplication │ ├── extract.js // Date/task extraction │ ├── score.js
  // Priority scoring algorithm │ └── classify.js // Final tier
  assignment ├── ai/ │ └── groq.js // Groq API wrapper ├── alerts/ │ └──
  whatsappAlert.js // Self-message on critical ├── routes/ │ └── api.js
  // REST endpoints for frontend └── cron/ └── scheduler.js // 15-min
  polling cron jobs

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------

**2.2 Data Flow (Full Pipeline)**

1.  Cron job fires every 15 minutes (Outlook) or 30 minutes (ERP)

2.  Connector fetches raw messages/notices and stores in Firestore with
    status: \'raw\'

3.  Pipeline worker picks up all \'raw\' messages and runs deduplication
    (SHA-256 hash)

4.  Extractor runs regex patterns first, then falls back to Groq API for
    ambiguous text

5.  Scorer computes priority score (0--100) using the formula defined in
    PRD Section 3.3

6.  Classifier assigns tier and confidence flag. Items below 70%
    confidence → \'review\' status

7.  Final processed message stored in Firestore with status:
    \'processed\'

8.  Frontend polls /api/messages endpoint every 5 minutes for fresh data

9.  If any new Critical item found, alert connector sends WhatsApp
    message to user

**2.3 Microsoft Graph API --- Outlook Integration**

-   Auth: OAuth 2.0 with scopes: Mail.Read, User.Read

-   Endpoint: GET /me/messages --- fetches last 50 unread messages per
    poll

-   Token refresh: Access tokens expire in 1 hour --- refresh token
    stored securely in Firebase

-   Rate limit: 10,000 requests/10 minutes --- our polling frequency is
    well within limits

**2.4 WhatsApp Integration (Prototype)**

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  Important: whatsapp-web.js is an unofficial library. It works for demo
  purposes but requires a persistent Node.js process. If WhatsApp updates
  their web client, this may break temporarily.

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------

-   Session: Browser session managed by Puppeteer, persists via
    LocalAuth strategy

-   Selective monitoring: User configures which chat IDs to watch ---
    stored in Firebase settings

-   Message handler: onMessage event listener --- only processes
    messages from selected chats

-   Self-alert: client.sendMessage(userNumber, alertText) for critical
    notifications

**2.5 ERP Scraper**

-   Tool: Puppeteer with headless Chromium

-   Pages scraped: Notices board, Fee payment status, Exam schedule

-   Session: Login credentials stored in environment variables (.env),
    never in code

-   Fragility acknowledgement: If ERP changes its HTML structure,
    selectors must be updated manually

**3. AI Processing Layer**

**3.1 Groq API Usage**

Model: llama3-8b-8192 (fast, sufficient for classification tasks)

Three separate prompts are used in the pipeline:

-   Extraction prompt: Given raw email text, extract: (1) deadline date
    if any, (2) task description in one line, (3) any monetary
    amounts, (4) location/venue. Respond in JSON only.

-   Classification prompt: Given extracted fields, determine category
    from: \[deadline, placement, meeting, fee, exam, event, notice\].
    Respond with category and confidence (0--100) only.

-   Summary prompt: Summarise this message in 2 sentences for a busy
    student. Highlight the most important fact.

**3.2 Confidence Handling**

If Groq returns confidence \< 70% on any field, that field is marked as
uncertain. If the overall item confidence is \< 70%, the entire card is
routed to the \'Needs Review\' section of the dashboard. The raw message
body is always preserved so users can read it in full.

**3.3 Fake News / Misinformation Handling**

-   Self-report rule: Announcements about a sender\'s own organisation
    are trusted (e.g. Anthropic reporting on Claude is trusted
    directly).

-   Third-party claim rule: If Source A makes a claim about Organisation
    B, confidence is reduced by 20 points.

-   Cross-source verification: If 2+ independent official sources
    confirm the same event, confidence is boosted to 95+.

-   Re-verification: Source URLs are re-fetched every 6 hours. If
    content changes or is deleted, the card is flagged.

**4. Frontend Architecture**

**4.1 Component Structure**

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  nexus-frontend/ ├── components/ │ ├── Masthead.jsx // Date, sources,
  last updated │ ├── TodaysBriefing.jsx // Newspaper-style lead + stories
  │ ├── TodaysSchedule.jsx // Timeline of calendar events │ ├──
  DeadlineTracker.jsx // Grouped deadline countdown │ └── NeedsReview.jsx
  // Uncertain items + manual input ├── hooks/ │ ├── useMessages.js //
  Polls /api/messages every 5 min │ └── useSettings.js // User
  preferences from Firebase └── utils/ └── priorityHelpers.js // Color,
  label, icon mapping

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------

**4.2 State Management**

No Redux. React useState + useContext is sufficient for this project
scope. All server state managed via the custom useMessages hook with
5-minute polling interval.

**5. Database Schema (Firestore)**

  ---------------- ----------------- -------------------------------------
  **Collection**   **Document ID**   **Fields**

  messages         SHA-256 hash      source, sender, subject, body,
                                     receivedAt, priority, confidence,
                                     extractedTask, extractedDate,
                                     category, status

  settings         userId            watchedChats (array), alertNumber,
                                     digestTime, erpCredentials
                                     (encrypted)

  deadlines        auto-id           title, summary, dueDate, category,
                                     source, addedManually, completedAt
  ---------------- ----------------- -------------------------------------

**6. Environment Variables**

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  \# Microsoft Graph API MS_CLIENT_ID= MS_CLIENT_SECRET= MS_REDIRECT_URI=
  \# Groq GROQ_API_KEY= \# Firebase FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON= \#
  WhatsApp WHATSAPP_USER_NUMBER= \# ERP ERP_USERNAME= ERP_PASSWORD=

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  CRITICAL: Never commit .env to GitHub. Add to .gitignore immediately.
  Use Railway environment variables panel for production.

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------

**NEXUS**

Product Requirements Document

*Personal AI Intelligence Layer for IIT Students*

+-----------------+-----------------+-----------------+-----------------+
| **Author**      | **Version**     | **Date**        | **Status**      |
|                 |                 |                 |                 |
| P. Sree Sai     | 1.0             | March 2026      | Draft           |
| Pavan           |                 |                 |                 |
+-----------------+-----------------+-----------------+-----------------+

**1. Problem Statement**

Every IIT student manages information scattered across at least three
platforms: college Outlook email, WhatsApp groups, and the institute
ERP. Critical deadlines --- assignment submissions, exam schedules, fee
payments, internship PPTs --- arrive through all three simultaneously,
often with no consolidation.

The result: students miss important announcements not because they are
careless, but because the information architecture forces them to
context-switch between multiple noisy channels throughout the day.

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  Core insight: The problem is not lack of information --- it is lack of
  prioritisation. Students receive enough notices. They just cannot tell
  which ones matter, when.

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------

**1.1 Pain Points (Validated)**

-   Critical deadlines buried inside long email threads

-   Same announcement forwarded 5--10 times across WhatsApp groups ---
    noise without signal

-   ERP notices not pushed via any notification --- require manual login
    to discover

-   No single view of \'what do I need to do today and this week\'

-   Internship-related updates (PPTs, registration deadlines) are
    particularly high-stakes and frequently missed

**1.2 Who This Is Built For**

Primary user: B.Tech students at IIT Guwahati, specifically those in
their 2nd and 3rd year when internship and academic pressures peak
simultaneously.

Secondary user: Any university student with a similar multi-platform
information problem. The architecture is institution-agnostic once the
data connectors are replaced.

**2. Product Vision**

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  Nexus is a personal AI intelligence layer that reads your Outlook,
  monitors selected WhatsApp chats, and scrapes your college ERP --- then
  surfaces only what matters, as a clean daily newspaper every morning.

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------

The product is not a notification manager. It is not another inbox. It
is a synthesis layer --- it reads everything so you do not have to, and
tells you only what requires your attention.

**2.1 Design Principles**

-   Nothing is ever silently dropped. Low-confidence items are shown in
    a separate review section.

-   Transparency over automation. Every card shows its confidence score
    and source.

-   Zero disturbance. One morning digest plus real-time alerts only for
    critical items.

-   Mobile-first. Students check phones first --- the dashboard must
    work perfectly on mobile.

-   Newspaper metaphor. Information is presented as editorial content,
    not raw data.

**3. Feature Specifications**

**3.1 Data Sources (MVP)**

  --------------- ---------------------- ----------------- ---------------
  **Source**      **Method**             **Frequency**     **Status**

  Outlook Email   Microsoft Graph API    Every 15 min      MVP
                  (OAuth)                                  

  WhatsApp Groups whatsapp-web.js        Real-time         MVP
                  (selected chats)                         

  College ERP     Puppeteer scraper      Every 30 min      MVP
                  (2--3 pages)                             

  ERP Exam        Puppeteer scraper      Daily             MVP
  Schedule                                                 
  --------------- ---------------------- ----------------- ---------------

**3.2 Classification Engine**

Every ingested message is processed through a 6-step pipeline:

1.  Ingest: Raw message fetched and stored with metadata (source,
    sender, timestamp)

2.  Deduplicate: SHA hash of subject + sender + date. Duplicate messages
    from multiple sources are merged into one card.

3.  Extract: Regex + LLM (Groq API) extracts dates, task names, amounts,
    locations, and deadlines from unstructured text.

4.  Score: Priority score computed using keyword weights, deadline
    proximity, sender trust, and category base weights.

5.  Classify: Score mapped to priority tier --- Critical / Important /
    Normal / Low.

6.  Confidence flag: Items with extraction confidence below 70% are
    routed to the \'Needs Review\' section instead of the main feed.

**3.3 Priority Scoring Formula**

  ------------------ --------------------------------- --------- -------------
  **Factor**         **Description**                   **Max     **Example**
                                                       Score**   

  Keyword Match      deadline, urgent, mandatory, fee, 40        fee → 40pts
                     exam, internship...                         

  Deadline Proximity \<24hrs=35, \<3days=25,           35        \<24hrs →
                     \<7days=15, \>7days=5                       35pts

  Sender Trust       Official college domain = high    15        iitg.ac.in →
                     trust                                       15pts

  Category Base      Fee/Placement/Exam \> Meeting \>  10        Placement →
                     Notice                                      10pts
  ------------------ --------------------------------- --------- -------------

Score ≥ 80 → Critical \| 50--79 → Important \| 20--49 → Normal \| \< 20
→ Low

**3.4 Dashboard Sections**

-   Today\'s Briefing --- Newspaper-style. Lead story is highest
    priority item. Tap to expand full summary + highlight.

-   Today\'s Schedule --- Timeline of all events for the current day,
    ordered chronologically.

-   Deadlines --- Grouped by urgency: Today → Tomorrow → In 2 Days → and
    so on, sorted by proximity.

-   Needs Your Review --- Items Nexus could not confidently classify.
    User can manually add a deadline to any item.

**3.5 Alert System**

-   Critical items: WhatsApp message sent to user\'s own number within 2
    minutes of detection.

-   Daily digest: Morning summary sent at 8:00 AM every day.

-   Nothing sent for Normal or Low priority items --- user checks
    dashboard at their own pace.

**4. Non-Functional Requirements**

**Accuracy target:** ≥ 80% for deadline extraction on explicit dates.
Vague language (e.g. \'before the break\') is routed to Needs Review
section.

**Latency:** Dashboard load \< 2 seconds. Critical alert delivery \< 2
minutes from source.

**Reliability:** WhatsApp integration is prototype-grade (may
disconnect). Outlook and ERP integrations must be stable.

**Privacy:** All data stored in user\'s own Firebase instance. Nothing
sent to third-party servers except Groq API for summarisation.

**Availability:** Backend hosted on Railway (always-on). Frontend on
Vercel.

**5. Out of Scope (MVP)**

-   Two-way actions (replying to emails, sending WhatsApp messages on
    user\'s behalf)

-   Calendar integration (Google Calendar, Outlook Calendar)

-   Multi-user / team features

-   Mobile app (React web with mobile-responsive design is sufficient
    for MVP)

-   Full ERP scraping --- only 3 specific pages (notices, fee, exam
    schedule) in MVP

**6. Success Metrics**

Since this is a personal/CV project, success is measured by:

-   All 4 dashboard sections rendering correctly with real data from
    Outlook

-   At least one working WhatsApp alert demonstrable in a video

-   ERP scraper fetching 3 pages without manual intervention

-   Mobile-responsive layout working on all screen sizes

-   Live deployed URL accessible without any setup from the reviewer\'s
    side
